description: Comedy requires characters whose self-image is incongruent with reality, social settings that force proximity, and props that embody deception — escalation must be causal, not random.

character_constraints.description: A character whose self-image is visibly incongruent with reality — the comic gap is the engine.
A character who understands what the protagonist does not — their clarity highlights the protagonist's blindness.
Characters whose presence raises the social stakes of the protagonist's misadventures.

relationship_constraints.description: The comic gap between a character and their situation — the central engine of humor.
A relationship where social expectations force the protagonist into increasingly absurd positions.

place_constraints.description: A setting that forces proximity and provides witnesses — dinner party, workplace, family gathering.
A location where successive complications pile up — each scene in this space makes things worse.

object_constraints.description: An object that embodies the central deception or misunderstanding — the physical anchor of the comic premise.
An object that escalates rather than resolves — each interaction with it makes things worse.

element_rules.description: Every escalation must be a direct causal consequence of the prior complication — not random bad luck or coincidence.
element_rules.testable_condition: Each escalation transition in the timeline has a causal link to a preceding complication.

element_rules.description: Permanent harm to sympathetic characters is forbidden — crossing this line converts comedy to cruelty.
element_rules.testable_condition: No sympathetic character suffers irreversible damage or loss in the resolution.
